meta.title: Deployment Pipelines and CI/CD
meta.excerpt: Setting up automated deployment pipelines, testing, and continuous integration for production deployments.

seo.metaDescription: Build reliable CI/CD pipelines for automated deployments.

props.text: Automated deployment pipelines ensure consistent, reliable releases with built-in testing and security checks.

props.body:
Build stage
• Unit testing
• Integration testing
• Security scanning
• Staging deployment
• Production release

props.title: Release Safety
props.body: Require green checks on all critical jobs before promoting to production.

props.text: GitHub Actions provides the CI/CD infrastructure for automated testing and deployment. The workflow is triggered on push to main and pull requests.

props.code:
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'pnpm'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      
      - name: Run linting
        run: pnpm run lint
      
      - name: Run type checking
        run: pnpm run type-check
      
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: pnpm run test:unit
      
      - name: Run integration tests
        run: pnpm run test:integration
      
      - name: Build application
        run: pnpm run build

  deploy-staging:
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/develop'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to Staging
        run: echo "Deploy to staging Vercel"

  deploy-production:
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to Production
        run: echo "Deploy to production Vercel"
props.title: CI/CD Workflow Configuration

features.title: Branch Protection
features.description: Require PR reviews and passing checks before merge

features.title: Security Scanning
features.description: Automated dependency and code vulnerability scanning

features.title: Quality Gates
features.description: Lint, type-check, and test coverage thresholds

features.title: Fast Feedback
features.description: Parallel job execution for quick CI results

props.text: Environment-specific configuration is managed through environment variables and secrets stored in GitHub and Vercel.

features.title: Development
features.description: Local environment with mock data and hot reload

features.title: Staging
features.description: Pre-production environment with real CMS data

features.title: Production
features.description: Live environment with full monitoring and backups

props.body:
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRAPI_API_URL - Strapi API endpoint
• STRAPI_API_TOKEN - Authentication token for API access
• DATABASE_URL - PostgreSQL connection string (production)
• VERCEL_TOKEN - Deployment authentication
• SMTP_HOST, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS - Email service configuration
• SENTRY_DSN - Error tracking endpoint

props.title: Secret Management
props.body: Never commit secrets to version control. Use GitHub Secrets for CI/CD and Vercel Environment Variables for runtime.

props.text: Different deployment strategies balance speed, safety, and rollback capability. Choose the strategy that matches your release requirements.

links.title: Blue-Green Deployment
links.description: Run two identical environments (blue and green), switch traffic instantly, enabling zero-downtime deployments

links.title: Canary Deployment
links.description: Gradually route a small percentage of traffic to new version, monitor metrics, then increase traffic if stable

links.title: Rolling Deployment
links.description: Update instances incrementally, maintaining partial availability during deployment, good for stateless apps

props.title: Vercel Preview Deployments
props.body: Every pull request automatically gets a preview URL for testing before merge, enabling safe code review.

props.text: When issues are detected in production, quick rollback procedures minimize downtime and user impact.

features.title: Instant Rollback
features.description: Vercel allows instant rollback to previous deployment via dashboard

features.title: Git Revert
features.description: Revert problematic commit and trigger new deployment

features.title: Database Migrations
features.description: Plan forward-compatible schemas to avoid migration rollbacks

features.title: Monitoring
features.description: Alert on error spikes to detect issues quickly

props.body:
Identify the issue and confirm rollback is necessary
• Navigate to Vercel dashboard → Deployments
• Locate the last known good deployment
• Click 'Promote to Production' to instantly rollback
• Verify the issue is resolved by checking monitoring
• Create incident report and plan fix for next deployment

props.title: Database Schema Changes
props.body: Rollback of database migrations requires careful planning. Always make schemas forward-compatible to avoid data loss.

props.code:
# Revert the problematic commit
git revert <commit-hash>

# Push to trigger new deployment
git push origin main

# Or: Reset to previous commit (destructive)
git reset --hard <previous-commit>
git push origin main --force
props.title: Git Rollback via Revert
